About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker Dashboard Today's data shows two exceptional performers dominating the momentum rankings. ELA ranks #1 with a composite score of 96.6, driven by extraordinary returns (292% over 1 year, 87.5% over 3 months) while maintaining perfect positioning above all major moving averages (SMA 30/50/100/200). EFXT follows closely with a 294% one-year return and all five ratio consistency checks passing, indicating sustained momentum across every timeframe interval. Both tickers demonstrate remarkable resilience on down SPY days, with ELA scoring 99.7 and EFXT scoring only 1.5 on the bad_spy_score metric, meaning they held strength when the broader market weakened. The key distinction is ratio consistency: EFXT's ratios are more uniform across timeframes (5 passes vs ELA's 4), while ELA shows a dramatic spike in the 1-month-to-1-week ratio (17.93), suggesting recent acceleration in momentum. Both names are correlated with bond and credit markets (LQD, TLT, HYG) and negatively correlated with commodities and energy (DBA, XLE), indicating a growth-oriented sector rotation rather than broad market strength.
